title: LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS - 211info
text: FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people to housing programs, including shelter information, other housing resources, tenant support, and non-housing resources. Does not provide emergency housing or rental assistance, and there may be a waitlist. 4060 W Amazon Drive Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Families with minor children who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: 7 days per week 9am-5pm (541) 342-7728 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 342-7728 Main phone Main phone (541) 342-7728 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in or call for appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Lane County SITE HOURS: 7 days per week 9am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located on LTD bus routes 24, 28, and 36. Services: Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Homeless Families Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER HOUSING/SHELTER - NIGHT SHELTER ANNEX HOUSING/SHELTER - OVERNIGHT PARKING PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER
